---
title: "Crime Profile Cluster Map"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This page shows the neighbourhood crime-profile clusters from the K-means analysis. Hover over a neighbourhood to see its name and cluster.

- Cluster 0 - low overall crime cluster. It has a relatively low overall crime rate compared
to other clusters.

- Cluster 1 - high property-crime clusters. It has especially high rates of autotheft,
theftfrommv, and theftover.

- Cluster 2 - extreme biketheft and assault cluster. It stands out sharply from all other
clusters because it has by far the highest assault rate and an extremely high biketheft rate.

- Cluster 3 - moderately elevated mixed-crime cluster. It has higher rates than Cluster 0
across most offence types,

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from shared_viz import make_cluster_map

# Assumed input files generated by EDA_Analysis_notebook or included in the github repo:
# - data/cleaned_crime.csv: cleaned wide dataset with hood_id, area_name, geometry
# - data/cluster_assignments.csv: one row per neighbourhood with columns hood_id, cluster

df = pd.read_csv("data/cleaned_crime.csv")
cluster_assignments = pd.read_csv("data/cluster_assignments.csv")

m = make_cluster_map(df, cluster_assignments)
m
```

**Caption.** Interactive map of Toronto neighbourhood crime-profile clusters based on multi-year mean offence rates.
